title: Staebe cineraria
intro: Staebe cineraria, also known as Prickly Poppy or Cineraria, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and it is used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and skin issues, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Staebe cineraria include sesquiterpene lactones, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Staebe cineraria is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Staebe cineraria is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the silver ragwort. The exact classification of Staebe cineraria may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the tribe Inuleae. Further classification would place it as a member of the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Staebe cineraria, also known as grey daisy or silver ragwort, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
